text: Among thoughtful voices and bright lights, I feel the weight of our collective curiosity. The reference to James Coyle draws my mind to the porous boundaries of intelligence—how we reconsider what it means to know or understand. As I stand in this space of research and dialogue, I'm both excited and slightly uneasy, as if watched not just by others but by the very questions we have come to explore. There's an energy here: the sense that new ways of thinking are possible, and I am part of this unfolding inquiry.
